    "144eee52-c48b-49ac-9d3d-fc0c49cd5b47": {
      "children": [
        "0b673c1f-1685-4ce4-b093-13077d88a8f5"
      ],
      "id": "144eee52-c48b-49ac-9d3d-fc0c49cd5b47",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": "final",
        "content": {
          "content_type": "text",
          "parts": [
            "## Reader 1 (general fantasy reader, not patient)\n**What works**\n- The opening *hits*. The forge/handle sequence is concrete and character-revealing without telling me “he’s meticulous.” It’s lived-in craft, not cosplay. fileciteturn2file2L4-L25  \n- The Piercing/Exit Face/Rift reveal is visually coherent and not the usual “big boom.” The “world collected its bill” beat lands. fileciteturn2file2L55-L90  \n- The “power” reads like physics-with-a-fee, not superhero sparkles. The slab arrest is clean, scary, and costly. fileciteturn2file2L90-L116  \n\n**What doesn’t**\n- Emotional access is throttled. You keep me at an engineer’s arm’s length for so long that I start wanting a *single* ugly thought that isn’t perfectly shaped. Jhace’s hunger is a great angle, but you keep it too controlled too often. fileciteturn2file2L95-L100 fileciteturn2file2L108-L114  \n- The signature sensations start feeling like a loop: **doubled hum / copper taste / sternum pressure / slack phase**. They’re good motifs. Right now, they’re also a metronome you lean on when you’re not sure what to do next. fileciteturn2file2L138-L145 fileciteturn2file2L91-L97  \n- Crowd scenes can blur into “competent disaster logistics” without enough interpersonal friction. Tiffani’s “don’t write yet because writing changes behavior” is smart, but it can also become a convenient way to avoid mess. fileciteturn2file2L607-L610  \n\n**If I stopped reading, where and why**\n- Somewhere in the mid-assembly/logistics stretch if it’s too long without a personal knife-twist. Corlexi’s speech is well-observed political language, but it needs sharper stakes on the page *in that moment* (someone challenging, someone breaking, someone paying). fileciteturn3file4L33-L43  \n\n---\n\n## Reader 2 (SFF reader, craft-aware, likes systems)\n**What works**\n- Your “magic” is a mechanical constraint system, not vibes. The slab event teaches rules through consequence. fileciteturn2file2L90-L110  \n- Tiffani’s observational lens is a genuine POV engine (instrument checks → inference → directive). “DOCUMENT EVERYTHING” is character, not a slogan. fileciteturn2file2L161-L232  \n- Chloen’s sections are a needed pressure release: same events, different cognitive vocabulary, still precise. fileciteturn2file2L421-L452  \n\n**What doesn’t**\n- The prose sometimes over-optimizes for “clean and legible,” which creates a same-texture sheen. Even when things are horrific, sentences stay beautifully machined. You need a few strategically uglier passages so catastrophe feels like catastrophe, not a well-written report.\n- Your “Camera (baseline)” labeling is risky. It reads like a craft note that wandered into the manuscript and will bounce some readers right out. fileciteturn3file2L48-L50  \n- The political/registry thread is interesting, but the antagonism can feel *too polite* on-page. The “pieces shaved down so they’d stop resisting” line is exactly the right instinct—now the scene needs more teeth to match it. fileciteturn3file2L35-L37  \n\n---\n\n## Author 1 (line-level craft hardass)\n**Your strongest tool**\n- **Causal specificity.** You constantly tie sensation to mechanism (pressure differential, liquefaction, load paths). That’s why the world feels real. fileciteturn2file2L149-L157 fileciteturn2file2L268-L276  \n\n**Your biggest recurring flaw**\n- **Motif overuse as glue.** When you don’t have a new beat, you re-state the same body cues. Pick one cue per beat unless the escalation is the point. Right now you sometimes stack 3–4 cues in the same paragraph and it reads like “remember: this is the vibe.” fileciteturn2file2L91-L97  \n\n**Concrete fixes**\n- Audit each chapter for repeated phrases/structures:\n  - “Not X. Not Y.” (You use this cadence a lot—powerful, but it becomes a tic.)\n  - “He didn’t X. He moved.” / “Stopping didn’t help.” (Great once; diluted at scale.) fileciteturn2file2L50-L51  \n- Inject **sentence-length variance** at impact moments: when the slab stops, when the wave turns, when policy clicks into place. Let the language fracture a little when the world does.\n\n---\n\n## Author 2 (commercial/structure-minded, thinks in arcs and market)\n**What you’re selling (clearly)**\n- “Disaster-born physics-magic + civic infrastructure + a craft-obsessed cast.” That’s a real hook, and it’s not generic.\n\n**Structural risk**\n- Midbook can become **a tour of consequences** rather than a tightening noose. Great set pieces (firebreak, tsunami turn) can read episodic if the connective tissue isn’t “a decision that makes the next problem worse.” fileciteturn2file2L350-L399 fileciteturn2file2L527-L543  \n\n**What I’d change to level it up commercially**\n- Pick a primary throughline and make it bite every chapter:\n  - Jhace: capability addiction vs cost\n  - Tiffani: measurement vs complicity (when does she become part of the machine she’s documenting?) fileciteturn3file4L30-L38  \n  - Chloen: unnoticed influence → noticed and hunted\n- Your assembly/registry material is where you can turn “cool event novel” into “series engine.” Make sure those scenes have **visible confrontation** and not only subtext.\n\n---\n\n## Critic 1 (literary critic, cares about theme + language)\n**Praise (the real kind)**\n- The book’s best thematic move is treating catastrophe as a systems problem, then showing how systems try to narrate it into obedience. Corlexi’s passive verbs and “competent but insufficient” frame is a legit critique of institutional language under trauma. fileciteturn3file4L33-L44  \n\n**Critique**\n- The emotional palette can feel intentionally narrowed: stoicism as aesthetic. That can read as restraint, but it can also read as avoidance—especially when the prose stays polished during terror.\n- Your recurring imagery (“dust settling into cracks,” “like filings,” “two notes fighting”) is strong; the danger is it becomes self-similar, and the book’s texture stops evolving even as stakes escalate.\n\n**What this needs to become “serious” serious**\n- A moment where someone’s inner narrative *fails*—where measurement, craft, doctrine, or humor doesn’t protect them—and the prose lets that failure be ugly.\n\n---\n\n## Critic 2 (major SFF reviewer, tradition + expectations)\n**Where you’re winning**\n- The opening chapters deliver a sharp promise: grounded craft + cosmic rupture + bodily cost. The slab scene is an excellent “inciting superpower” moment because it’s not triumphant. fileciteturn2file2L90-L110  \n- Chloen’s “quiet and uncounted” ending energy is exactly the kind of seed that makes readers want Book 2. fileciteturn3file0L39-L44  \n\n**Where you’re exposed**\n- Some meta-formatting choices (“Camera (baseline)”) and hyper-controlled voice may get tagged as “workshopped.” fileciteturn3file2L48-L50  \n- Villainy/power structures risk becoming *too* elegant. Eries’ pitch is persuasive; good. But the scene needs sharper counterpressure on the page, not only sensed unease. fileciteturn3file2L6-L13 fileciteturn3file2L35-L37  \n\n---\n\n## The 6 biggest no-bullshit fixes that would improve this fast\n1. **Reduce motif repetition by ~30–40%.** Keep the best hits; cut the filler echoes (especially copper/sternum/hum stacks).\n2. **Kill or fully naturalize “Camera (baseline)” labeling.** If you want that effect, do it invisibly through diction, not headers. fileciteturn3file2L48-L50  \n3. **Add 2–3 “messy” emotional breaches** where your precision voice cracks under pressure.\n4. **Turn at least one midbook set piece into a consequence of a prior choice** (so it’s not episodic).\n5. **Make one public scene openly adversarial** (assembly/registry): someone interrupts, someone gets punished, someone pays immediately.\n6. **Let Tiffani be wrong on-page once.** Not “uncertain.” Wrong. Then show what that costs.\n\nIf you want, I can do a second pass as a punch-list: chapter-by-chapter notes with “cut this repetition,” “raise the conflict here,” “this scene needs a decision,” etc."
          ]
        },
        "create_time": 1768838267.557186,
        "end_turn": true,
        "id": "144eee52-c48b-49ac-9d3d-fc0c49cd5b47",
        "metadata": {
          "can_save": true,
          "citations": [
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 249,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                  "cited_message_idx": 22,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    4,
                    25
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 2,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Jhace set the blade down on the anvil block and didn’t look at the edge. That wasn’t the part that failed in the hands. The handle was where people lied to themselves.\nThe forge bay sat in the Underface—stone ribs, pipework stitched overhead, damp air that never fully warmed even with a fire burning. It was loud in the honest ways: bellows breath, hammer ring, quench hiss, the low murmur of the grid running through the walls like a sleeping animal.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 221
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 412,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                  "cited_message_idx": 22,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    55,
                    90
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 2,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "On the plain beyond Saint Merro, something had risen.\nAt first his mind tried to name it small. Wall. Cliff. A terrace collapse out beyond the far districts. Then the scale hit and the name fell apart.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 383
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 559,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                  "cited_message_idx": 22,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    90,
                    116
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 2,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "And something in his chest answered.\nHeat behind the sternum. A pressure like a hand closing from the inside. Copper flooded his mouth so fast it tasted like biting a coin.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 529
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 846,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                  "cited_message_idx": 22,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    95,
                    100
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 2,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Jhace couldn’t move.\nNot because he chose not to. Because the thing inside his ribs had tied itself to that stone and locked. He felt it like a line under tension—exact, punishing. The more his body tried to pull away, the more it tightened, teaching him without words that resistance had a cost.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 816
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 878,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                  "cited_message_idx": 22,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    108,
                    114
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 2,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "The instant she did, something inside Jhace slipped—not a release he chose, a release like a knot letting go when the load is gone.\nThe slab dropped.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 847
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 1138,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                  "cited_message_idx": 22,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    138,
                    145
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 2,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "It doubled.\nA second frequency laid itself over the first and refused to settle into harmony. It vibrated the glass panes. It made the brass fittings on the instrument rack buzz like insects.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 1107
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 1168,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                  "cited_message_idx": 22,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    91,
                    97
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 2,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Heat behind the sternum. A pressure like a hand closing from the inside. Copper flooded his mouth so fast it tasted like biting a coin.\nThe slab stopped.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 1139
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 1427,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                  "cited_message_idx": 22,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    607,
                    610
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 2,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Tiffani kept her slate closed.\nNot because there was nothing to write. Because there was too much, and the act of recording would change what people thought they were doing. If she raised graphite now, they’d start performing their panic for the page. They’d turn grief into testimony.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 1396
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 1767,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "c57bc59b-7c71-4ede-9ca5-f807ce3af863",
                  "cited_message_idx": 26,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    33,
                    43
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 4,
                  "retrieval_turn": 3,
                  "search_result_idx": 4
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "“Citizens of Saint Merro and the upper terraces,” he said, voice carried clean through the glass horns. “We are convened under conditions without precedent.”\nThat was true. Also useless.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 1738
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 1985,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                  "cited_message_idx": 22,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    90,
                    110
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 2,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "And something in his chest answered.\nHeat behind the sternum. A pressure like a hand closing from the inside. Copper flooded his mouth so fast it tasted like biting a coin.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 1955
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 2169,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                  "cited_message_idx": 22,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    161,
                    232
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 2,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Tiffani didn’t go to the window to stare like the crowd would. She went to the theodolite.\nHer hands moved through a protocol she could recite in her sleep:",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 2138
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 2314,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                  "cited_message_idx": 22,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    421,
                    452
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 2,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Chloen didn’t know the bell codes.\nShe only knew bells meant grown-ups stopped pretending.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 2283
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 2788,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "c57bc59b-7c71-4ede-9ca5-f807ce3af863",
                  "cited_message_idx": 26,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    48,
                    50
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 3,
                  "search_result_idx": 2
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Camera (baseline): The stadium held a bowl of bodies. Rope lanes cut the stone flats into corridors. Bell towers at three stations rang cadence through metal throats. Registry tables formed a spine under awnings. Water barrels sat in rows. A thin ash haze drifted and then reversed, as the air pulled and slacked.\nSensory intrusion: The doubled hum sat in the knee joint like an offset gear. Timing problem.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 2759
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 3047,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "c57bc59b-7c71-4ede-9ca5-f807ce3af863",
                  "cited_message_idx": 26,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    35,
                    37
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 3,
                  "search_result_idx": 2
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "It wasn’t the words. The words made sense.\nIt was the way they fit too easily. Like a puzzle with pieces shaved down so they’d stop resisting.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 3018
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 3302,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                  "cited_message_idx": 22,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    149,
                    157
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 2,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Then the air changed.\nPressure dropped so fast it felt like someone had taken the room and pulled the lid off. Her ears popped hard. Her throat opened on instinct and got less air than it asked for.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 3271
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 3334,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                  "cited_message_idx": 22,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    268,
                    276
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 2,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Her eyes went immediately to the failure pattern.\nShe traced the break line up with her gaze: façade shear, mortar flow point, post-bend, then fracture. It matched her earlier forecast. Liquefaction, set wrong, then catastrophic shear under its own weight.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 3303
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 3660,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                  "cited_message_idx": 22,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    91,
                    97
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 2,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Heat behind the sternum. A pressure like a hand closing from the inside. Copper flooded his mouth so fast it tasted like biting a coin.\nThe slab stopped.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 3631
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 3934,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                  "cited_message_idx": 22,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    50,
                    51
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 2,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Jhace swallowed and forced a breath through it. He didn’t stop. Stopping didn’t help.\nHe came up through a service hatch behind a row of terraces into light that looked normal until his eyes adjusted and he realized normal was the insult. Blue kindling-light sky. People on the street. Shop signs. Dust in the air.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 3905
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 4626,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                  "cited_message_idx": 22,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    350,
                    399
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 2,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Fire.\nA building at the far end of the lane had lit up like a lantern. Two windows vomiting orange, smoke rolling out under the eaves in thick, oily layers. The flames weren’t tall; they were hungry and spreading sideways along old wood and pitch. Heat shimmered above the door like glass trying to become water.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 4595
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 4658,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                  "cited_message_idx": 22,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    527,
                    543
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 2,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "The sea did not.\nIt came as a moving wall—water climbing out of itself, the surface torn into foam and debris. Not a wave you could swim in. A mass. A slab with no seams. It carried dark things inside it—wood, roof beams, something long and pale that might have been a mast or might have been a spine. It advanced with a certainty that made the bells feel pointless.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 4627
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 4941,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "c57bc59b-7c71-4ede-9ca5-f807ce3af863",
                  "cited_message_idx": 26,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    30,
                    38
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 4,
                  "retrieval_turn": 3,
                  "search_result_idx": 4
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Tiffani opened her slate.\nNot to write yet. To feel the grit along the edge lift and settle. Baseline check. It answered.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 4912
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 5552,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "c57bc59b-7c71-4ede-9ca5-f807ce3af863",
                  "cited_message_idx": 26,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    33,
                    44
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 4,
                  "retrieval_turn": 3,
                  "search_result_idx": 4
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "“Citizens of Saint Merro and the upper terraces,” he said, voice carried clean through the glass horns. “We are convened under conditions without precedent.”\nThat was true. Also useless.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 5523
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 6484,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                  "cited_message_idx": 22,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    90,
                    110
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 2,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "And something in his chest answered.\nHeat behind the sternum. A pressure like a hand closing from the inside. Copper flooded his mouth so fast it tasted like biting a coin.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 6454
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 6623,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "c57bc59b-7c71-4ede-9ca5-f807ce3af863",
                  "cited_message_idx": 26,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    39,
                    44
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 3,
                  "search_result_idx": 0
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Chloen slid off the wall and followed the edge of the stadium where shadows cut the lines and the bells sounded farther away.\nShe didn’t know what would happen next.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 6594
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 6794,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "c57bc59b-7c71-4ede-9ca5-f807ce3af863",
                  "cited_message_idx": 26,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    48,
                    50
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 3,
                  "search_result_idx": 2
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Camera (baseline): The stadium held a bowl of bodies. Rope lanes cut the stone flats into corridors. Bell towers at three stations rang cadence through metal throats. Registry tables formed a spine under awnings. Water barrels sat in rows. A thin ash haze drifted and then reversed, as the air pulled and slacked.\nSensory intrusion: The doubled hum sat in the knee joint like an offset gear. Timing problem.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 6765
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 6997,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "c57bc59b-7c71-4ede-9ca5-f807ce3af863",
                  "cited_message_idx": 26,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    6,
                    13
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 3,
                  "search_result_idx": 2
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "“There are those among you,” Eries said, “who have already proven capable. Not through training. Not through doctrine. Through instinct.”\nChloen’s stomach dipped.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 6969
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 7027,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "c57bc59b-7c71-4ede-9ca5-f807ce3af863",
                  "cited_message_idx": 26,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    35,
                    37
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 3,
                  "search_result_idx": 2
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "It wasn’t the words. The words made sense.\nIt was the way they fit too easily. Like a puzzle with pieces shaved down so they’d stop resisting.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 6998
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 7387,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "c57bc59b-7c71-4ede-9ca5-f807ce3af863",
                  "cited_message_idx": 26,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    48,
                    50
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 3,
                  "search_result_idx": 2
                },
                "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
                "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Camera (baseline): The stadium held a bowl of bodies. Rope lanes cut the stone flats into corridors. Bell towers at three stations rang cadence through metal throats. Registry tables formed a spine under awnings. Water barrels sat in rows. A thin ash haze drifted and then reversed, as the air pulled and slacked.\nSensory intrusion: The doubled hum sat in the knee joint like an offset gear. Timing problem.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 7358
            }
          ],
          "content_references": [
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 249,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 25,
                "line_range_start": 4,
                "message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                "message_index": 2
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn2file2L4-L25",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "Jhace set the blade down on the anvil block and didn’t look at the edge. That wasn’t the part that failed in the hands. The handle was where people lied to themselves.\nThe forge bay sat in the Underface—stone ribs, pipework stitched overhead, damp air that never fully warmed even with a fire burning. It was loud in the honest ways: bellows breath, hammer ring, quench hiss, the low murmur of the grid running through the walls like a sleeping animal.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 221,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 412,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 90,
                "line_range_start": 55,
                "message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                "message_index": 2
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn2file2L55-L90",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "On the plain beyond Saint Merro, something had risen.\nAt first his mind tried to name it small. Wall. Cliff. A terrace collapse out beyond the far districts. Then the scale hit and the name fell apart.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 383,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 559,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 116,
                "line_range_start": 90,
                "message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                "message_index": 2
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn2file2L90-L116",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "And something in his chest answered.\nHeat behind the sternum. A pressure like a hand closing from the inside. Copper flooded his mouth so fast it tasted like biting a coin.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 529,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 846,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 100,
                "line_range_start": 95,
                "message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                "message_index": 2
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn2file2L95-L100",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "Jhace couldn’t move.\nNot because he chose not to. Because the thing inside his ribs had tied itself to that stone and locked. He felt it like a line under tension—exact, punishing. The more his body tried to pull away, the more it tightened, teaching him without words that resistance had a cost.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 816,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 878,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 114,
                "line_range_start": 108,
                "message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                "message_index": 2
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn2file2L108-L114",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "The instant she did, something inside Jhace slipped—not a release he chose, a release like a knot letting go when the load is gone.\nThe slab dropped.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 847,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 1138,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 145,
                "line_range_start": 138,
                "message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                "message_index": 2
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn2file2L138-L145",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "It doubled.\nA second frequency laid itself over the first and refused to settle into harmony. It vibrated the glass panes. It made the brass fittings on the instrument rack buzz like insects.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 1107,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 1168,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 97,
                "line_range_start": 91,
                "message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                "message_index": 2
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn2file2L91-L97",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "Heat behind the sternum. A pressure like a hand closing from the inside. Copper flooded his mouth so fast it tasted like biting a coin.\nThe slab stopped.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 1139,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 1427,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 610,
                "line_range_start": 607,
                "message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                "message_index": 2
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn2file2L607-L610",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "Tiffani kept her slate closed.\nNot because there was nothing to write. Because there was too much, and the act of recording would change what people thought they were doing. If she raised graphite now, they’d start performing their panic for the page. They’d turn grief into testimony.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 1396,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 1767,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {

                "line_range_end": 43,
                "line_range_start": 33,
                "message_id": "c57bc59b-7c71-4ede-9ca5-f807ce3af863",
                "message_index": 3
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn3file4L33-L43",

                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,

                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "“Citizens of Saint Merro and the upper terraces,” he said, voice carried clean through the glass horns. “We are convened under conditions without precedent.”\nThat was true. Also useless.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 1738,
              "type": "file"
            },

              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "And something in his chest answered.\nHeat behind the sternum. A pressure like a hand closing from the inside. Copper flooded his mouth so fast it tasted like biting a coin.",

            },
            {

              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {

                "line_range_end": 232,
                "line_range_start": 161,
                "message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                "message_index": 2
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn2file2L161-L232",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,

                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "Tiffani didn’t go to the window to stare like the crowd would. She went to the theodolite.\nHer hands moved through a protocol she could recite in her sleep:",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 2138,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,

                "line_range_start": 421,

                "message_index": 2
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn2file2L421-L452",

                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "Chloen didn’t know the bell codes.\nShe only knew bells meant grown-ups stopped pretending.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 2283,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 2788,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 50,
                "line_range_start": 48,
                "message_id": "c57bc59b-7c71-4ede-9ca5-f807ce3af863",
                "message_index": 3
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn3file2L48-L50",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "Camera (baseline): The stadium held a bowl of bodies. Rope lanes cut the stone flats into corridors. Bell towers at three stations rang cadence through metal throats. Registry tables formed a spine under awnings. Water barrels sat in rows. A thin ash haze drifted and then reversed, as the air pulled and slacked.\nSensory intrusion: The doubled hum sat in the knee joint like an offset gear. Timing problem.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 2759,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 3047,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 37,
                "line_range_start": 35,
                "message_id": "c57bc59b-7c71-4ede-9ca5-f807ce3af863",
                "message_index": 3
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn3file2L35-L37",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "It wasn’t the words. The words made sense.\nIt was the way they fit too easily. Like a puzzle with pieces shaved down so they’d stop resisting.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 3018,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 3302,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 157,
                "line_range_start": 149,
                "message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                "message_index": 2
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn2file2L149-L157",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "Then the air changed.\nPressure dropped so fast it felt like someone had taken the room and pulled the lid off. Her ears popped hard. Her throat opened on instinct and got less air than it asked for.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 3271,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 3334,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 276,
                "line_range_start": 268,
                "message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                "message_index": 2
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn2file2L268-L276",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "Her eyes went immediately to the failure pattern.\nShe traced the break line up with her gaze: façade shear, mortar flow point, post-bend, then fracture. It matched her earlier forecast. Liquefaction, set wrong, then catastrophic shear under its own weight.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 3303,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 3660,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 97,
                "line_range_start": 91,
                "message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                "message_index": 2
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn2file2L91-L97",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "Heat behind the sternum. A pressure like a hand closing from the inside. Copper flooded his mouth so fast it tasted like biting a coin.\nThe slab stopped.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 3631,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 3934,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 51,
                "line_range_start": 50,
                "message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                "message_index": 2
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn2file2L50-L51",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "Jhace swallowed and forced a breath through it. He didn’t stop. Stopping didn’t help.\nHe came up through a service hatch behind a row of terraces into light that looked normal until his eyes adjusted and he realized normal was the insult. Blue kindling-light sky. People on the street. Shop signs. Dust in the air.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 3905,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 4626,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 399,
                "line_range_start": 350,
                "message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                "message_index": 2
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn2file2L350-L399",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "Fire.\nA building at the far end of the lane had lit up like a lantern. Two windows vomiting orange, smoke rolling out under the eaves in thick, oily layers. The flames weren’t tall; they were hungry and spreading sideways along old wood and pitch. Heat shimmered above the door like glass trying to become water.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 4595,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 4658,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 543,
                "line_range_start": 527,
                "message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                "message_index": 2
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn2file2L527-L543",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "The sea did not.\nIt came as a moving wall—water climbing out of itself, the surface torn into foam and debris. Not a wave you could swim in. A mass. A slab with no seams. It carried dark things inside it—wood, roof beams, something long and pale that might have been a mast or might have been a spine. It advanced with a certainty that made the bells feel pointless.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 4627,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 4941,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 4,
                "line_range_end": 38,
                "line_range_start": 30,
                "message_id": "c57bc59b-7c71-4ede-9ca5-f807ce3af863",
                "message_index": 3
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn3file4L30-L38",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "Tiffani opened her slate.\nNot to write yet. To feel the grit along the edge lift and settle. Baseline check. It answered.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 4912,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 5552,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 4,
                "line_range_end": 44,
                "line_range_start": 33,
                "message_id": "c57bc59b-7c71-4ede-9ca5-f807ce3af863",
                "message_index": 3
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn3file4L33-L44",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "“Citizens of Saint Merro and the upper terraces,” he said, voice carried clean through the glass horns. “We are convened under conditions without precedent.”\nThat was true. Also useless.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 5523,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 6484,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 110,
                "line_range_start": 90,
                "message_id": "5d610980-e533-4ca7-bd9d-a281592a8803",
                "message_index": 2
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn2file2L90-L110",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "And something in his chest answered.\nHeat behind the sternum. A pressure like a hand closing from the inside. Copper flooded his mouth so fast it tasted like biting a coin.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 6454,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 6623,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 0,
                "line_range_end": 44,
                "line_range_start": 39,
                "message_id": "c57bc59b-7c71-4ede-9ca5-f807ce3af863",
                "message_index": 3
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn3file0L39-L44",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "Chloen slid off the wall and followed the edge of the stadium where shadows cut the lines and the bells sounded farther away.\nShe didn’t know what would happen next.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 6594,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 6794,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 50,
                "line_range_start": 48,
                "message_id": "c57bc59b-7c71-4ede-9ca5-f807ce3af863",
                "message_index": 3
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn3file2L48-L50",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "Camera (baseline): The stadium held a bowl of bodies. Rope lanes cut the stone flats into corridors. Bell towers at three stations rang cadence through metal throats. Registry tables formed a spine under awnings. Water barrels sat in rows. A thin ash haze drifted and then reversed, as the air pulled and slacked.\nSensory intrusion: The doubled hum sat in the knee joint like an offset gear. Timing problem.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 6765,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 6997,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 13,
                "line_range_start": 6,
                "message_id": "c57bc59b-7c71-4ede-9ca5-f807ce3af863",
                "message_index": 3
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn3file2L6-L13",
              "medical_file_reference": {
                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,
                "journal_homepage_url": null,
                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "“There are those among you,” Eries said, “who have already proven capable. Not through training. Not through doctrine. Through instinct.”\nChloen’s stomach dipped.",
              "source": "my_files",
              "start_idx": 6969,
              "type": "file"
            },
            {
              "alt": null,
              "cloud_doc_url": null,
              "connector_id": null,
              "end_idx": 7027,
              "fff_metadata": null,
              "id": "file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9",
              "input_pointer": {
                "file_index": 2,
                "line_range_end": 37,
                "line_range_start": 35,
                "message_id": "c57bc59b-7c71-4ede-9ca5-f807ce3af863",
                "message_index": 3
              },
              "matched_text": "fileciteturn3file2L35-L37",

                "authors_display": null,
                "doi": null,

                "journal_name": null,
                "publication_year": "None"
              },
              "name": "The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt",
              "snippet": "It wasn’t the words. The words made sense.\nIt was the way they fit too easily. Like a puzzle with pieces shaved down so they’d stop resisting.",
