It answered.\n[L22] Corlexi began.\n[L23] “Citizens of Saint Merro and the upper terraces,” he said, voice carried clean through the glass horns. “We are convened under conditions without precedent.”\n[L24] That was true. Also useless.\n[L25] “We have experienced a planetary structural failure event,” he continued. “External in origin. Sudden. Unpreventable.”\n[L26] External. Tiffani underlined it in her mind and waited for the evidence that would follow.\n[L27] “It has compromised the grid,” Corlexi said. “Altered pressure dynamics. Introduced sustained harmonic instability.”\n[L28] He was careful with verbs. Passive constructions. No actor named.\n[L29] The crowd stayed quiet. Too quiet. People leaned forward a fraction with each pulse, then settled back on the slack. They had learned the rhythm faster than she’d expected.\n[L30] Corlexi spoke of logistics.\n[L31] Assembly points. Rationing. Repair priorities. The need for order while assessment continued. He named the glass-houses—plural—and acknowledged the Underface without promising aid he couldn’t deliver.\n[L32] All of it was competent.\n[L33] All of it was insufficient.\n[L34] Tiffani waited for him to say how big.\n[L35] She waited for where else.\n[L36] She waited for him to acknowledge the hole that ran through the planet like a throat.\n[L37] He did not.\n[L38] Around her, reactions registered in micro-movements.\n[L39] Relief, where people heard “external” and translated it to not our fault.\n[L40] Anxiety, where “assessment ongoing” sounded like no answers yet.\n[L41] Bram stood two rows down, assigned to the aisle. He leaned on his spear like it was a prop, jaw tight, eyes bright with the effort of paying attention. He nodded at the right places. When Corlexi spoke about “citizen cooperation,” Bram’s shoulders eased, just a touch.\n[L42] Jhace did not nod.\n[L43] He stood back from the risers, weight on his heels, arms folded like he was bracing a wall.\n# 【2†The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt†file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn21file2\nMclick Target: \"21:2\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] Ruin was honest. Ruin paid you back quickly. Ruin didn’t pretend to forgive.\r\n[L2] Purity had taken him in prison because Purity offered something ruin never did: a line you could walk and not hate yourself every step.\r\n[L3] He didn’t pretend Purity made him good. Purity made him intentional.\r\n[L4] “I don’t know,” Mobel said. “Not yet.”\r\n[L5] Kerrin’s gaze stayed steady. “You moved a wall of water.”\r\n[L6] Mobel’s throat worked. “I redirected it.”\r\n[L7] “Still.”\r\n[L8] Mobel closed his eyes for half a breath and felt the phantom of it again—the door, the access, the terrifying scope.\r\n[L9] He opened his eyes.\r\n[L10] “Purity,” he said, and the word tasted like both prayer and chain. “If I’m anything, I’m that. Because what I felt wasn’t destruction. It wasn’t shaping. It wasn’t harmony. It was… cleansing force. A current. A pressure. A straightening.”\r\n[L11] Kerrin nodded, slow. “Purity aligns.”\r\n[L12] Mobel’s jaw tightened. “Purity also burns.”\r\n[L13] Kerrin didn’t disagree.\r\n[L14] Mobel’s fingers curled once on his knee, a small involuntary grip like he was holding a handle that wasn’t there.\r\n[L15] He pictured the ocean again, obeying. He pictured taking it and setting it down gentle as cloth. He pictured failing and drowning the city anyway.\r\n[L16] He tasted metallic again, and this time the annoyance rose hot.\r\n[L17] Stupid metal.\r\n[L18] He forced a breath on the slack phase.\r\n[L19] In. Out.\r\n[L20] Then he looked at Kerrin.\r\n[L21] “If it happens again,” Mobel said, “don’t let me get pretty with it.”\r\n[L22] Kerrin’s expression didn’t shift. “Pretty.”\r\n[L23] “Don’t let me make a show,” Mobel said. “Don’t let me forget what I am.”\r\n[L24] Kerrin’s answer was immediate. Familiar. A promise given before, in different forms.\r\n[L25] “I won’t,” he said.\r\n[L26] Outside, the terrace worklines kept moving. Stone passed hand to hand. Names called. Rope tightened. The world held itself together with labor and contradiction.\r\n[L27] Mobel sat at the edge of the room and listened to the doubled hum inside the wall, and tried to decide whether feeling alive was a blessing or just a different kind of sentence.\r\n[L28] Chapter 11 - Tiffani (The Cadence)\r\n[L29] The bells shifted cadence.\r\n[L30] Three short strikes. A pause. One long strike. Two short.\r\n[L31] Stone carried it clean. Pipe ribs carried it wrong, vibrating the pattern into extra notes. The doubled hum laid under the metal like a second hand trying to write over the first.\r\n[L32] People stopped moving. Heads turned toward the nearest station tower.\r\n[L33] Tiffani stopped too. Slate under her arm. Pencil in her fingers. She counted the spacing, not the meaning.\r\n[L34] Three. Pause. Long. Two.\r\n[L35] Station notice.\r\n[L36] Not collapse warning. Not fire. Not brace call.\r\n[L37] The long strike meant: listen.\r\n[L38] A runner climbed the tower steps and pinned a sheet to the board below the bell yoke. Another runner held the corners when the wind pulled.\r\n[L39] Tiffani watched the paper flap, then watched the hands that pinned it. Copper-gold grit lifted along the slate edge and settled again.\r\n[L40] Jhace was two paces to her right. Wedge bar on his shoulder like a yoke. His hands stayed low. Visible.\r\n[L41] Chloen stayed close, small shadow tucked into the gap between their bodies and the crowd.\r\n[L42] The bell tower runner shouted.\r\n[L43] \"By Crown order. Assembly at the Basin Stadium. Live address. Corlexi of Infrastructure.\n# 【3†confluence_novella_revised.md†file_00000000a47071fdaf1ac40e7e480e92】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: confluence_novella_revised.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn21file3\nMclick Target: \"21:3\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] The guard captain wore a tab with the crown mark at the collar.\n[L2] A group of workers in slate-dust cloaks stood near the names table. Chalk in hand. Slates under arm. They watched hands, not faces.\n[L3] A message runner arrived from the upper terrace with a sealed tube. The tube was passed to a clerk, then to the factor.\n[L4] The factor broke the seal. The paper was read. The paper was folded.\n[L5] On the platform, an administrator in a clean coat stepped to the speaking rail. A glass horn was adjusted to align with his mouth.\n[L6] At the names table, a clerk dipped a charcoal stick and wrote faster.\n[L7] A bell struck once. A pause.\n[L8] The stadium bowl quieted.\n[L9] Chapter 14 — Tiffani (The Briefing)\n[L10] The stadium was not built for this many bodies at once.\n[L11] It had been built for contests.\n[L12] Now the stone tiers held families and crates and brace timber. People sat with tools across their knees because putting tools down felt like letting go of the only reliable thing.\n[L13] Tiffani took a position where she could see the platform and one of the main aisle lines. She kept her slate on her lap. Closed. Not because she was not recording. Because she was choosing what would not be stolen.\n[L14] The horn-glass amplifiers had been assembled on the platform: three flared cones of smoked glass mounted in iron frames, their throats wrapped in cloth to reduce vibration. Copper wire bracing ran from the frames to anchors drilled into the stone.\n[L15] The wire shivered on the doubled hum.\n[L16] A bell cadence ran again from the nearest station tower, shorter this time, a confirmation sequence.\n[L17] Three short. One long. One short.\n[L18] Attention.\n[L19] A crown guard line tightened along the platform edge. Interns were mixed in with full guards. Bram was easy to identify by absence of stillness. He shifted, then corrected, then shifted again like he was trying to find a posture that did not look like a boy wearing someone else's authority.\n[L20] Jhace stood one tier down and two rows over, wedge bar tucked under his arm like a crutch he refused to admit he needed. Chloen was beside him, shoulders drawn in, eyes scanning for exits even though this place had too many.\n[L21] A man stepped onto the platform.\n[L22] Clean coat. Straight seams. An iron emblem pinned at the collar: a square frame with a single diagonal bar. Form.\n[L23] He did not wait for applause.\n[L24] He placed both hands on the podium rail and began.\n[L25] \"Notice from the Crown Council on Infrastructure and Public Safety,\" he said.\n[L26] The horn-glass carried his voice across stone. It arrived in fragments where the hum interfered and clean where the cloth wraps held.\n[L27] \"Chief Administrator Corlexi,\" he continued. \"Office of Infrastructure and Public Works. Forty-eight hour situation report. Verified items only.\"\n[L28] Tiffani opened her slate.\n[L29] She wrote the time interval in the corner and drew a line under it.\n[L30] Corlexi spoke in numbered sections.\n[L31] \"One: Communication status. The long-range grid is nonfunctional in most territories.\" He paused, allowing the crowd to hear the phrase most territories and supply its own meaning. \"Metal infrastructure is carrying the new harmonic. It is not usable for transmission. Attempts to force signal through have resulted in line fracture and fire.\"\n[L32] Tiffani wrote: GRID 98% FAIL (REPORT). CONDUCTOR BEHAVIOR.\n[L33] \"Local bell networks remain effective within city limits. Cadence corruption has been observed. If your station tower issues overlapping sequences, defer to the longest strike. The long strike indicates verified notice.\"\n[L34] A brace captain two tiers down nodded once, sharp, repeating the instruction to the men near him.\n# 【4†confluence_novella_revised.md†file_00000000a47071fdaf1ac40e7e480e92】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: confluence_novella_revised.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn21file4\nMclick Target: \"21:4\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] The guard captain wore a tab with the crown mark at the collar.\n[L2] A group of workers in slate-dust cloaks stood near the names table. Chalk in hand. Slates under arm. They watched hands, not faces.\n[L3] A message runner arrived from the upper terrace with a sealed tube. The tube was passed to a clerk, then to the factor.\n[L4] The factor broke the seal. The paper was read. The paper was folded.\n[L5] On the platform, an administrator in a clean coat stepped to the speaking rail. A glass horn was adjusted to align with his mouth.\n[L6] At the names table, a clerk dipped a charcoal stick and wrote faster.\n[L7] A bell struck once. A pause.\n[L8] The stadium bowl quieted.\n[L9] Chapter 14 — Tiffani (The Briefing)\n[L10] The stadium was not built for this many bodies at once.\n[L11] It had been built for contests.\n[L12] Now the stone tiers held families and crates and brace timber. People sat with tools across their knees because putting tools down felt like letting go of the only reliable thing.\n[L13] Tiffani took a position where she could see the platform and one of the main aisle lines. She kept her slate on her lap. Closed. Not because she was not recording. Because she was choosing what would not be stolen.\n[L14] The horn-glass amplifiers had been assembled on the platform: three flared cones of smoked glass mounted in iron frames, their throats wrapped in cloth to reduce vibration. Copper wire bracing ran from the frames to anchors drilled into the stone.\n[L15] The wire shivered on the doubled hum.\n[L16] A bell cadence ran again from the nearest station tower, shorter this time, a confirmation sequence.\n[L17] Three short. One long. One short.\n[L18] Attention.\n[L19] A crown guard line tightened along the platform edge. Interns were mixed in with full guards. Bram was easy to identify by absence of stillness. He shifted, then corrected, then shifted again like he was trying to find a posture that did not look like a boy wearing someone else's authority.\n[L20] Jhace stood one tier down and two rows over, wedge bar tucked under his arm like a crutch he refused to admit he needed. Chloen was beside him, shoulders drawn in, eyes scanning for exits even though this place had too many.\n[L21] A man stepped onto the platform.\n[L22] Clean coat. Straight seams. An iron emblem pinned at the collar: a square frame with a single diagonal bar. Form.\n[L23] He did not wait for applause.\n[L24] He placed both hands on the podium rail and began.\n[L25] \"Notice from the Crown Council on Infrastructure and Public Safety,\" he said.\n[L26] The horn-glass carried his voice across stone. It arrived in fragments where the hum interfered and clean where the cloth wraps held.\n[L27] \"Chief Administrator Corlexi,\" he continued. \"Office of Infrastructure and Public Works. Forty-eight hour situation report. Verified items only.\"\n[L28] Tiffani opened her slate.\n[L29] She wrote the time interval in the corner and drew a line under it.\n[L30] Corlexi spoke in numbered sections.\n[L31] \"One: Communication status. The long-range grid is nonfunctional in most territories.\" He paused, allowing the crowd to hear the phrase most territories and supply its own meaning. \"Metal infrastructure is carrying the new harmonic. It is not usable for transmission. Attempts to force signal through have resulted in line fracture and fire.\"\n[L32] Tiffani wrote: GRID 98% FAIL (REPORT). CONDUCTOR BEHAVIOR.\n[L33] \"Local bell networks remain effective within city limits. Cadence corruption has been observed. If your station tower issues overlapping sequences, defer to the longest strike. The long strike indicates verified notice.\"\n[L34] A brace captain two tiers down nodded once, sharp, repeating the instruction to the men near him.\n# 【5†confluence_novella_revised.md†file_00000000a47071fdaf1ac40e7e480e92】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: confluence_novella_revised.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn21file5\nMclick Target: \"21:5\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] He leaned close to the doorway’s edge where the steam had cooled the stone enough to touch without skin sticking.\n[L2] He didn’t go in yet.\n[L3] He listened.\n[L4] Through the wet hiss and the bell cadence and the planet’s doubled hum, he heard something inside the building that wasn’t fire.\n[L5] A cough. Human. Close.\n[L6] Jhace set his jaw.\n[L7] He raised his hands again—smaller this time—and shaped the air to hold the smoke back from the doorway like a curtain.\n[L8] His sternum burned.\n[L9] He did it anyway.\n[L10] Chapter 5 — Chloen (The Child Under the Slab)\n[L11] Chloen didn’t know the bell codes.\n[L12] She only knew bells meant grown-ups stopped pretending.\n[L13] It started like a feeling in her teeth.\n[L14] Not pain. A buzzing, like when you put your ear too close to the pipe runs in the Underface and the whole world sounds like it’s inside a shell. She was on the mid-terrace steps with a bundle of rags under one arm because Aunt Lessa had sent her down to fetch clean wrap from the stitch shop, and Chloen had tried to walk like she wasn’t late.\n[L15] Then the air got thin.\n[L16] Not cold. Thin.\n[L17] Her ears popped so hard it made her blink, and when she tried to take a bigger breath to fix it, her chest didn’t fill right—like someone had pinched the air.\n[L18] Smoke did something wrong above the rooftops. It didn’t rise. It pulled sideways and down, sucked back into chimneys as if the houses were drinking it.\n[L19] She stopped on the step and watched it, because her brain didn’t know what else to do.\n[L20] People in the street stopped too.\n[L21] Not all at once. It was like a slow ripple—one person turning, then another, then everyone’s heads going the same way.\n[L22] A cart horse stamped and tried to back up, eyes white.\n[L23] Chloen held her rag bundle tighter and followed the line of their faces.\n[L24] Far away past the terraces, something was happening on the plain. At first it looked like a storm wall, the kind that comes low and fast and eats the sky.\n[L25] Then it got taller. And taller. And it didn’t move like weather.\n[L26] It rose.\n[L27] A wall. A cliff. A line where the world had been flat and now it wasn’t. It climbed straight up, banded like the layers in cut rock—light, dark, light—except the layers were standing upright like someone had taken the ground and turned it on its side.\n[L28] Someone beside her whispered a prayer. Someone else laughed once, sharp, the wrong kind of laugh.\n[L29] The bell went again—one street over—fast, uneven.\n[L30] Chloen’s stomach felt like it had dropped through her shoes.\n[L31] And then the sky tore.\n[L32] There was suddenly a hole in it. Not a cloud hole. A clean, black place in the blue.\n[L33] Stars inside it.\n[L34] In the day.\n[L35] That made her throat close even before the air tried to leave again.\n[L36] The grown-ups made a sound like one big breath being stolen.\n[L37] Chloen stared so hard her eyes watered. The black place shimmered at the edge, like the air was shaking because it didn’t know where to stand.\n[L38] Something moved in that black.\n[L39] Too fast. Too long.\n[L40] She saw a flash of… not metal, not stone, not anything she’d ever held. It crossed the hole like a needle pulled through cloth. There were colors along it—bright veins that blinked copper and gold and green and another color that made her eyes hurt to try and name.\n[L41] Then it was gone.\n[L42] But the after of it wasn’t.\n[L43] A streak of broken things followed behind it. Some pieces kept going with it. Some pieces fell back.\n[L44] And that was when Chloen’s feet started to feel wrong.\n# 【6†The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt†file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn21file6\nMclick Target: \"21:6\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] Jhace held his hands out and kept sweeping, smaller motions now, compressing and directing. He wasn’t blowing the fire out. He was smothering it, stripping it of oxygen and drowning it in wet heat.\r\n[L2] His sternum locked. Breath cut. Vision narrowed.\r\n[L3] Copper flooded his mouth.\r\n[L4] He forced the motion anyway. Tiny adjustments. Keep the steam sheet low. Keep it in the door. Don’t let it backwash onto the crowd.\r\n[L5] The fire fought once—hungry, bright—then choked again as the steam packed the doorway.\r\n[L6] The orange dwindled to a pulse behind wet smoke.\r\n[L7] Jhace’s arms trembled from shoulder to fingertips. Not effort like lifting a hammer—effort like holding a vibration in place while your bones tried to resonate with it.\r\n[L8] His lungs finally dragged in a thin breath. It tasted like copper and wet ash.\r\n[L9] He let the wind go on the slack phase.\r\n[L10] The steam thinned. The smoke changed—less black, more gray. The doorway still radiated heat, but it wasn’t spitting flame anymore.\r\n[L11] Jhace staggered one step back and braced a hand on the stone wall because his knees had decided they were optional.\r\n[L12] He looked at his hands.\r\n[L13] They were shaking again, worse than before—tiny jerks, out of sync, as if his nerves were trying to find the doubled harmonic and missing.\r\n[L14] A cough tore out of him. Metal taste surged. He spat into the gutter without thinking and hated himself for it immediately.\r\n[L15] A woman pushed forward from the crowd, eyes wild. “Is it—?”\r\n[L16] “Not out,” Jhace said. “But it’s not eating the street. Get them away from the door. Now.”\r\n[L17] She stared at him like he’d grown new bones. Then she turned and started shouting directions, and some people listened because a loud voice in a crisis becomes law.\r\n[L18] Jhace wiped his mouth with his sleeve and swallowed the rest of the copper anyway.\r\n[L19] He leaned close to the doorway’s edge where the steam had cooled the stone enough to touch without skin sticking.\r\n[L20] He didn’t go in yet.\r\n[L21] He listened.\r\n[L22] Through the wet hiss and the bell cadence and the planet’s doubled hum, he heard something inside the building that wasn’t fire.\r\n[L23] A cough. Human. Close.\r\n[L24] Jhace set his jaw.\r\n[L25] He raised his hands again—smaller this time—and shaped the air to hold the smoke back from the doorway like a curtain.\r\n[L26] His sternum burned.\r\n[L27] He did it anyway.\r\n[L28] Chapter 5 — Chloen (The Child Under the Slab)\r\n[L29] Chloen didn’t know the bell codes.\r\n[L30] She only knew bells meant grown-ups stopped pretending.\r\n[L31] It started like a feeling in her teeth.\r\n[L32] Not pain. A buzzing, like when you put your ear too close to the pipe runs in the Underface and the whole world sounds like it’s inside a shell. She was on the mid-terrace steps with a bundle of rags under one arm because Aunt Lessa had sent her down to fetch clean wrap from the stitch shop, and Chloen had tried to walk like she wasn’t late.\r\n[L33] Then the air got thin.\r\n[L34] Not cold. Thin.\r\n[L35] Her ears popped so hard it made her blink, and when she tried to take a bigger breath to fix it, her chest didn’t fill right—like someone had pinched the air.\r\n[L36] Smoke did something wrong above the rooftops. It didn’t rise. It pulled sideways and down, sucked back into chimneys as if the houses were drinking it.\r\n[L37] She stopped on the step and watched it, because her brain didn’t know what else to do.\r\n[L38] People in the street stopped too.\r\n[L39] Not all at once.\n# 【7†The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt†file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn21file7\nMclick Target: \"21:7\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] He did not like sloppy.\r\n[L2] A guard captain in a crown tab passed and nodded at him once. \"Intern. Keep them moving.\"\r\n[L3] \"Yes, sir,\" Bram said.\r\n[L4] The captain moved on.\r\n[L5] Bram glanced back toward the section he'd placed them in.\r\n[L6] He found them by shape.\r\n[L7] Jhace: standing, wedge bar still in hand like a limb.\r\n[L8] Tiffani: sitting, slate closed on her knees, eyes forward.\r\n[L9] Chloen: small in the seat, shoulders tight, eyes too big for her face.\r\n[L10] Bram felt something in his chest that wasn't doubled hum and wasn't prayer. Just weight.\r\n[L11] He wanted to go back and say: sorry. He wanted to say: you don't have to answer me. He wanted to say: if you need water, I can get water.\r\n[L12] He didn't.\r\n[L13] Because guards didn't fix losses. Guards moved bodies.\r\n[L14] He kept walking the lanes.\r\n[L15] A bell struck the notice cadence again. Three short. Pause. One long. Two short.\r\n[L16] People quieted in waves.\r\n[L17] Bram stopped at the edge of a rope lane and watched the crowd settle into something like attention.\r\n[L18] He watched Chloen stand when the people around her stood, then sit when they sat, matching the tide of bodies with the same careful invisibility she'd carried all day.\r\n[L19] He watched her look once over her shoulder, not at him, just back at the path they'd walked as if checking whether the question had followed.\r\n[L20] He felt guilty anyway.\r\n[L21] He told himself he'd do better.\r\n[L22] He told himself he'd ask fewer questions.\r\n[L23] He told himself he'd keep his jokes aimed at the sky, not at kids.\r\n[L24] Then he saw the platform at the center of the stadium and the glass horns angled toward the stands, and he felt the larger truth settle in.\r\n[L25] Today was not going to stop handing him chances to fail.\r\n[L26] Chapter 13 - Camera (The Bowl)\r\n[L27] Bell stations at four corners of the stadium bowl rang the notice cadence in sequence.\r\n[L28] Guards raised ropes to open lanes. Guards lowered ropes to close lanes.\r\n[L29] Crowds entered by terrace ramps and split at chalk marks into sections. Cloth tabs tied to wrists matched section colors. Red, blue, grey, raw twine.\r\n[L30] A scaffold stood at the center platform: pipe frame, strap joints, wedge pins. Glass horns were mounted on crossbars, their mouths aimed at the stone tiers. A second set of horns faced outward to carry sound beyond the stadium.\r\n[L31] A table line formed behind the platform. Three tables. One for water cups. One for bandage cloth and salve tins. One for names.\r\n[L32] At the names table, clerks held ledgers and charcoal sticks. A runner stood beside them with a bag of wax tabs. A guard watched the table with a hand on a baton.\r\n[L33] Two brace captains walked the seating tiers with chalk, marking cracks and posting rope across unstable rows. A third brace captain stood near the center platform with a rolled map and a wedge bar.\r\n[L34] Pipe quarter engineers moved along the scaffold frame, tapping pipe with a rod, listening, then tightening strap joints. One engineer carried a small pressure gauge in a glass case. The needle jittered on the doubled hum.\r\n[L35] A forge-guild crew in soot-stained coats assembled a portable brazier near the center platform. They heated rivets. They drove them into a cracked crossbar. Steam hissed where water hit hot metal.\r\n[L36] Priests in four emblems stood at different edges of the platform.\r\n[L37] A square emblem on a clean cloth tab.\r\n[L38] A droplet emblem on a dark strip of leather.\r\n[L39] A loop emblem on braided cord.\r\n[L40] A fractured line emblem on a torn sash.\r\n[L41] They did not stand together.\r\n[L42] A factor with rings on both hands took a seat at the platform rail.\n# 【8†The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt†file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn21file8\nMclick Target: \"21:8\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] His posture read administrative fatigue as if it were a uniform requirement.\r\n[L2] Eries stood in the center.\r\n[L3] High class by cut and fit alone—no dust-stained hem, no patched seams, no improvised wrap. She wore a mantle that could have been ceremonial if it hadn’t been practical wool. That was the trick. She looked like she belonged and like she’d come to help.\r\n[L4] She didn’t raise her hands for silence. She waited until the bell cadence finished and let the last long note die.\r\n[L5] The stadium quieted on its own.\r\n[L6] Tiffani catalogued the quiet like a structural event.\r\n[L7] Eries spoke without amplification and still carried.\r\n[L8] “Your air is changing,” she said, and didn’t dress it in myth. “It will keep changing. The pull and slack will continue until the planet settles into a new baseline.”\r\n[L9] Baseline. Useful word. Correct enough to calm. Vague enough to hide scope.\r\n[L10] Tiffani’s pencil hand twitched. She kept it still.\r\n[L11] Eries continued, “You will not survive this by arguing with it.”\r\n[L12] She turned her head slightly, letting her gaze travel across the bowl so people could feel seen without being singled out.\r\n[L13] “Breathing protocols will be taught at every station,” Eries said. “The bell sequences will mark phase. When the cadence is short-short-long: shallow. When it is long-long-short: fill. You will practice. It will become habit. Habit is how bodies stop panicking.”\r\n[L14] Bram nodded before she finished. Not a conscious decision. Reflex agreement to a pattern that promised control.\r\n[L15] Jhace’s jaw set.\r\n[L16] Tiffani did not approve or reject. She evaluated.\r\n[L17] Bell cadence as phase training.\r\n[L18] If the cadence stayed true through harmonic interference, it could work. If it corrupted, it could kill.\r\n[L19] Eries didn’t stop there.\r\n[L20] “The grit,” she said, and a ripple moved through the crowd at the word. People touched their mouths. Their sleeves. Their tongues.\r\n[L21] Eries lifted a strip of cloth from her mantle—white, layered, folded tight. Not clean linen. Filter cloth. Industrial.\r\n[L22] “Wear it over your mouth,” she said. “Wet it. Replace it. The metallic particulate is conductive. It is in your lungs. It is in your teeth. We will reduce load.”\r\n[L23] Load. She was speaking their language on purpose.\r\n[L24] Tiffani’s attention sharpened. That wasn’t a priest’s vocabulary. That was infrastructure.\r\n[L25] Eries looked down at the first row of registry tables and pointed with two fingers, not dramatic, just directional.\r\n[L26] “Assembly points are now allocation points,” she said. “Food, water, heat access, brace crews. You will move through the lanes. You will be marked. You will be counted.”\r\n[L27] Counted.\r\n[L28] Tiffani watched the crowd absorb that.\r\n[L29] Some faces tightened. Some relaxed.\r\n[L30] Fear and relief were the same muscle, apparently. They just pulled in different directions.\r\n[L31] Eries added, “Those who experienced anomaly—those who held load, suspended objects, redirected flow—will be asked to step to the left lane. Not to punish you. To protect you.”\r\n[L32] Protect you. The softest lie. The most effective one.\r\n[L33] Tiffani’s knee registered a sharper pulse. She adjusted weight and pretended it was nothing.\r\n[L34] Chloen made a small noise behind her teeth—almost a snort, caught and swallowed.\r\n[L35] Jhace didn’t move toward any lane.\r\n[L36] Bram did. Half a step, then stopped like he remembered he wasn’t supposed to volunteer himself.\n# 【9†The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt†file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn21file9\nMclick Target: \"21:9\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] Bram didn’t clap, but his shoulders loosened like he’d set a pack down.\r\n[L2] Tiffani finally wrote.\r\n[L3] Just one line.\r\n[L4] Chloen couldn’t see the words, but she saw the graphite move around a darker smear already on the slate, like water avoiding oil.\r\n[L5] Jhace turned away.\r\n[L6] Not far. Just enough.\r\n[L7] Like he was done listening.\r\n[L8] Chloen stared at Eries and tried to figure out what felt wrong.\r\n[L9] It wasn’t the words. The words made sense.\r\n[L10] It was the way they fit too easily. Like a puzzle with pieces shaved down so they’d stop resisting.\r\n[L11] Chloen looked at the emblems stitched into coats and pinned to collars across the crowd. Square. Drop. Loop. Fracture.\r\n[L12] They weren’t where they’d been before.\r\n[L13] She didn’t know how she knew that.\r\n[L14] She just did.\r\n[L15] Chloen swallowed and tasted metal again—duller now, like it had been there a while.\r\n[L16] She leaned toward Tiffani’s side, not touching her, just close enough that if Tiffani moved, she’d feel it.\r\n[L17] “Don’t,” Chloen whispered. Not loud enough for the slate. Just for the space between them.\r\n[L18] Tiffani didn’t look down.\r\n[L19] But her pencil stopped.\r\n[L20] And Chloen watched Eries smile wider, like she’d felt the resistance and counted it anyway.\r\n[L21] Chapter 17 — Tiffani (Deployment)\r\n[L22] Camera (baseline): The stadium held a bowl of bodies. Rope lanes cut the stone flats into corridors. Bell towers at three stations rang cadence through metal throats. Registry tables formed a spine under awnings. Water barrels sat in rows. A thin ash haze drifted and then reversed, as the air pulled and slacked.\r\n[L23] Sensory intrusion: The doubled hum sat in the knee joint like an offset gear. Timing problem. Two beats where there should have been one.\r\n[L24] Tiffani stood where she could see the lanes and the stage without being part of either.\r\n[L25] She kept her slate tucked, not open. Open invited requests. Requests turned observation into labor. Labor made her miss the drift.\r\n[L26] Bells rang again—short, short, long—then a pause, then the same sequence repeated. Not panic cadence. Notice cadence. Central-station call.\r\n[L27] People reacted like trained metal. Heads lifted. Feet adjusted. Bodies shifted toward the lanes without anyone shouting at them.\r\n[L28] That was new.\r\n[L29] That was the first clean thing she’d seen since the sky tore.\r\n[L30] Bram moved along the nearest rope line with three other crown interns—too young for the way they were trying to carry authority, too stubborn to drop it. He had a sash pinned wrong and kept re-seating it as if fabric could be corrected into competence.\r\n[L31] He leaned down to a woman with a wrapped arm and pointed toward the nearest water barrel. His mouth moved. The woman nodded, then laughed once—short, unwilling. Bram’s shoulders loosened like he’d removed a pressure pin.\r\n[L32] Jhace stood off the line, wedge bar still in hand. Not working now. Watching. Refusing to be slotted into a lane.\r\n[L33] Chloen remained close enough to Tiffani that the crowd didn’t swallow her. Close enough that Tiffani could track her without turning it into a promise. The girl’s eyes kept jumping—rope, table, stage, back to rope—like she was mapping exits.\r\n[L34] The stage had been a platform for games. It was now a point of control.\r\n[L35] Corlexi had stepped down and taken a side position near the rear struts, half-shadowed by hanging banners. Form emblem at his collar. Clean coat. Too clean for the day.\nVisible: 0% - 100%",
          "summary": null,

    "e508b8ee-cfd3-491a-b2c3-a5b3e8444bb7": {
      "children": [
        "fd053b7e-5b14-413e-8b85-63f0428b53cb"
      ],
      "id": "e508b8ee-cfd3-491a-b2c3-a5b3e8444bb7",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "container.exec",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "     1\tJhace’s gaze stayed on the registrar’s hand. \"I said no.\"\n     2\t\n     3\tThe registrar’s smile held—thin, professional. \"Refusal is noted,\" he said, and pressed a wax tab into Bram’s palm instead. \"If he changes his mind, bring him to the left table.\"\n     4\t\n     5\tBram stared at the tab like it had weight. Tiffani did not write any of it.\n     6\tCorlexi's eyes moved over the stadium tiers like he was scanning a ledger.\n     7\t\"The Crown will hold daily briefings at central stations. Verification requires collected data. Speculation will be treated as a destabilizing force.\"\n     8\tForm principle in a single sentence: reduce unknowns, enforce shape.\n     9\tHe did not mention the orbital debris band.\n    10\tHe did not mention the hole through the planet.\n    11\tHe did not mention the way the grit lifted along slate edges near certain bodies.\n    12\tHe did not mention the ocean turning.\n    13\tHe said, \"We are alive. We are still functional. We will restore order through measured work.\"\n    14\tThen he stepped back from the podium.\n    15\tA crown guard approached the platform stairs. A registrar with a ledger moved into position at a side table.\n    16\tThe crowd began to stand.\n    17\tStone shifted under thousands of feet.\n    18\tThe hum tightened.\n    19\tTiffani felt it before she could name it. The slack phase did not arrive on schedule.\n    20\tHer knee caught on the expected beat and found none.\n    21\tA missed step.\n    22\tA stutter.\n    23\tBreath pulled shallow across the tiers in the same instant, as if the stadium had become one body and somebody had squeezed.\n    24\tMetal in mouths. Not copper. A hard clean hit.\n    25\tA brace timber in the lower aisle line creaked.\n    26\tThen the world skipped.\n    27\tA blank beat. A wrong beat. A hole in the rhythm.\n    28\tBram was on the aisle line, one hand on a rope post. His posture went wrong for a fraction, like a puppet whose string had slackened and snapped tight again. He recovered without falling. He did not understand what had happened. His eyes widened and then locked back into his assigned scan.\n    29\tA worker carrying a crate did not recover.\n    30\tHis foot landed on a stone lip that had been stable one beat ago. The lip shifted under the stutter. The crate tilted. His weight followed.\n    31\tHe went down.\n    32\tThe crate broke.\n    33\tGlass shards and iron bolts scattered.\n    34\tThe worker's forearm struck a jagged edge.\n    35\tBlood hit stone.\n    36\tTiffani's pencil moved without permission.\n    37\tBLOOD. LOCATION: AISLE 3. CAUSE: PHASE NULL / LOAD SHIFT.\n    38\tShe wrote around the red as if it were a stain on the page. Her lines tightened. Her letters narrowed.\n    39\tThe hum returned.\n    40\tThe slack phase arrived late.\n    41\tAir rushed back into lungs. The crowd noise surged, then splintered into shouts.\n    42\tGuards moved. Brace captains moved. Two medics pushed through with a cloth roll and a splint.\n    43\tBram's face went pale for one heartbeat, then he stepped aside to make a lane. He did not joke. He did not freeze. He held the rope post steady while bodies passed.\n    44\tJhace did not move toward the blood. He watched the brace timber and the shifting stone and the way the stutter had changed the crowd's balance. His hands flexed once, then clenched.\n    45\tChloen stood with her arms tight around herself, eyes on the aisle line. Her mouth worked once as if the metallic taste was too loud.\n    46\tCorlexi remained on the platform.\n    47\tHe did not return to the podium.\n    48\tHe did not speak.\n    49\tHis clean coat did not change.\n    50\tA registrar leaned in to say something to him. Corlexi nodded once, sharp, and pointed toward the side table.\n    51\tThe ledger opened.\n    52\tNames began to be taken.\n    53\tTiffani closed her slate.\n    54\tNot because the blood was not data.\n    55\tBecause the act of writing had just become part of the event.\n    56\tShe could feel the graphite under her fingers like a conductor.\n    57\tShe stood and shifted one step to spare her knee.\n    58\tShe did not look at Bram.\n    59\tShe did not look at Jhace.\n    60\tShe looked at the horn-glass amplifiers and the copper wire bracing and the way the wire shivered on the baseline as if it was listening.\n    61\tThen she exhaled on the slack phase and moved toward the aisle line where the blood had been, because measurement required proximity and proximity now carried cost.\n    62\tChapter 15 – Tiffani (The Omission)\n    63\tThe bells rang the Assembly cadence.\n    64\tNot alarm, evacuation, or mourning.\n    65\tInstruction.\n    66\tThree low. One high. Pause. Then the repeat—slightly off, like everything else now. The harmonic bled into the bronze, stretched the spacing between notes until the rhythm felt learned rather than remembered.\n    67\tTiffani stood where she could see the whole of it without being seen herself.\n    68\tThe crowd had been arranged for sound, not comfort. That was deliberate. Stone risers angled shallow toward the dais. Rope lines kept bodies from pressing too close to the glasswork that carried the amplification. Brace captains flanked the aisles, hands loose, eyes working.\n    69\tPeople had stopped talking before the bells finished.\n    70\tThat mattered.\n    71\tThey were waiting to be told what the Event was.\n    72\tCorlexi stood already at the center of the platform when the last echo died. No procession. No flourish. He wore Crown gray—clean lines, no ornament—and the way he held himself said he had practiced stillness until it read as calm instead of fear.\n    73\tTiffani opened her slate.\n    74\tNot to write yet. To feel the grit along the edge lift and settle. Baseline check. It answered.\n    75\tCorlexi began.\n    76\t“Citizens of Saint Merro and the upper terraces,” he said, voice carried clean through the glass horns. “We are convened under conditions without precedent.”\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1768841358.7010517,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "e508b8ee-cfd3-491a-b2c3-a5b3e8444bb7",

    "f17c8a91-a3b9-4e67-888b-17711ebdba36": {
      "children": [
        "2d6aff8a-b391-49d3-9d2a-875e6319c4ad"
      ],
      "id": "f17c8a91-a3b9-4e67-888b-17711ebdba36",
      "message": {
        "author": {

        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "assets": null,
          "content_type": "tether_browsing_display",
          "result": "# 【0†The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt†file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn16file0\nMclick Target: \"16:0\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] Guards raised ropes to open lanes. Guards lowered ropes to close lanes.\r\n[L2] Crowds entered by terrace ramps and split at chalk marks into sections. Cloth tabs tied to wrists matched section colors. Red, blue, grey, raw twine.\r\n[L3] A scaffold stood at the center platform: pipe frame, strap joints, wedge pins. Glass horns were mounted on crossbars, their mouths aimed at the stone tiers. A second set of horns faced outward to carry sound beyond the stadium.\r\n[L4] A table line formed behind the platform. Three tables. One for water cups. One for bandage cloth and salve tins. One for names.\r\n[L5] At the names table, clerks held ledgers and charcoal sticks. A runner stood beside them with a bag of wax tabs. A guard watched the table with a hand on a baton.\r\n[L6] Two brace captains walked the seating tiers with chalk, marking cracks and posting rope across unstable rows. A third brace captain stood near the center platform with a rolled map and a wedge bar.\r\n[L7] Pipe quarter engineers moved along the scaffold frame, tapping pipe with a rod, listening, then tightening strap joints. One engineer carried a small pressure gauge in a glass case. The needle jittered on the doubled hum.\r\n[L8] A forge-guild crew in soot-stained coats assembled a portable brazier near the center platform. They heated rivets. They drove them into a cracked crossbar. Steam hissed where water hit hot metal.\r\n[L9] Priests in four emblems stood at different edges of the platform.\r\n[L10] A square emblem on a clean cloth tab.\r\n[L11] A droplet emblem on a dark strip of leather.\r\n[L12] A loop emblem on braided cord.\r\n[L13] A fractured line emblem on a torn sash.\r\n[L14] They did not stand together.\r\n[L15] A factor with rings on both hands took a seat at the platform rail. Two assistants stood behind him with folded papers. A crown guard captain stood at his shoulder.\r\n[L16] A second guard captain walked the aisle lanes and pointed groups into sections. The guard captain wore a tab with the crown mark at the collar.\r\n[L17] A group of workers in slate-dust cloaks stood near the names table. Chalk in hand. Slates under arm. They watched hands, not faces.\r\n[L18] A message runner arrived from the upper terrace with a sealed tube. The tube was passed to a clerk, then to the factor.\r\n[L19] The factor broke the seal. The paper was read. The paper was folded.\r\n[L20] On the platform, an administrator in a clean coat stepped to the speaking rail. A glass horn was adjusted to align with his mouth.\r\n[L21] At the names table, a clerk dipped a charcoal stick and wrote faster.\r\n[L22] A bell struck once. A pause.\r\n[L23] The stadium bowl quieted.\r\n[L24] Chapter 14 - Tiffani (The Briefing)\r\n[L25] The stadium was not built for this many bodies at once.\r\n[L26] It had been built for contests.\r\n[L27] Now the stone tiers held families and crates and brace timber. People sat with tools across their knees because putting tools down felt like letting go of the only reliable thing.\r\n[L28] Tiffani took a position where she could see the platform and one of the main aisle lines. She kept her slate on her lap. Closed. Not because she was not recording. Because she was choosing what would not be stolen.\r\n[L29] The horn-glass amplifiers had been assembled on the platform: three flared cones of smoked glass mounted in iron frames, their throats wrapped in cloth to reduce vibration. Copper wire bracing ran from the frames to anchors drilled into the stone.\r\n[L30] The wire shivered on the doubled hum.\r\n[L31] A bell cadence ran again from the nearest station tower, shorter this time, a confirmation sequence.\r\n[L32] Three short. One long. One short.\r\n[L33] Attention.\r\n[L34] A crown guard line tightened along the platform edge. Interns were mixed in with full guards.\n# 【1†confluence_novella_revised.md†file_00000000a47071fdaf1ac40e7e480e92】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: confluence_novella_revised.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn16file1\nMclick Target: \"16:1\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] He did not understand what had happened. His eyes widened and then locked back into his assigned scan.\n[L2] A worker carrying a crate did not recover.\n[L3] His foot landed on a stone lip that had been stable one beat ago. The lip shifted under the stutter. The crate tilted. His weight followed.\n[L4] He went down.\n[L5] The crate broke.\n[L6] Glass shards and iron bolts scattered.\n[L7] The worker's forearm struck a jagged edge.\n[L8] Blood hit stone.\n[L9] Tiffani's pencil moved without permission.\n[L10] BLOOD. LOCATION: AISLE 3. CAUSE: PHASE NULL / LOAD SHIFT.\n[L11] She wrote around the red as if it were a stain on the page. Her lines tightened. Her letters narrowed.\n[L12] The hum returned.\n[L13] The slack phase arrived late.\n[L14] Air rushed back into lungs. The crowd noise surged, then splintered into shouts.\n[L15] Guards moved. Brace captains moved. Two medics pushed through with a cloth roll and a splint.\n[L16] Bram's face went pale for one heartbeat, then he stepped aside to make a lane. He did not joke. He did not freeze. He held the rope post steady while bodies passed.\n[L17] Jhace did not move toward the blood. He watched the brace timber and the shifting stone and the way the stutter had changed the crowd's balance. His hands flexed once, then clenched.\n[L18] Chloen stood with her arms tight around herself, eyes on the aisle line. Her mouth worked once as if the metallic taste was too loud.\n[L19] Corlexi remained on the platform.\n[L20] He did not return to the podium.\n[L21] He did not speak.\n[L22] His clean coat did not change.\n[L23] A registrar leaned in to say something to him. Corlexi nodded once, sharp, and pointed toward the side table.\n[L24] The ledger opened.\n[L25] Names began to be taken.\n[L26] Tiffani closed her slate.\n[L27] Not because the blood was not data.\n[L28] Because the act of writing had just become part of the event.\n[L29] She could feel the graphite under her fingers like a conductor.\n[L30] She stood and shifted one step to spare her knee.\n[L31] She did not look at Bram.\n[L32] She did not look at Jhace.\n[L33] She looked at the horn-glass amplifiers and the copper wire bracing and the way the wire shivered on the baseline as if it was listening.\n[L34] Then she exhaled on the slack phase and moved toward the aisle line where the blood had been, because measurement required proximity and proximity now carried cost.\n[L35] Chapter 15 – Tiffani (The Omission)\n[L36] The bells rang the Assembly cadence.\n[L37] Not alarm, evacuation, or mourning.\n[L38] Instruction.\n[L39] Three low. One high. Pause. Then the repeat—slightly off, like everything else now. The harmonic bled into the bronze, stretched the spacing between notes until the rhythm felt learned rather than remembered.\n[L40] Tiffani stood where she could see the whole of it without being seen herself.\n[L41] The crowd had been arranged for sound, not comfort. That was deliberate. Stone risers angled shallow toward the dais. Rope lines kept bodies from pressing too close to the glasswork that carried the amplification. Brace captains flanked the aisles, hands loose, eyes working.\n[L42] People had stopped talking before the bells finished.\n[L43] That mattered.\n[L44] They were waiting to be told what the Event was.\n[L45] Corlexi stood already at the center of the platform when the last echo died. No procession. No flourish. He wore Crown gray—clean lines, no ornament—and the way he held himself said he had practiced stillness until it read as calm instead of fear.\n[L46] Tiffani opened her slate.\n[L47] Not to write yet. To feel the grit along the edge lift and settle. Baseline check. It answered.\n[L48] Corlexi began.\n# 【2†confluence_novella_revised.md†file_00000000a47071fdaf1ac40e7e480e92】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: confluence_novella_revised.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn16file2\nMclick Target: \"16:2\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] The guard captain wore a tab with the crown mark at the collar.\n[L2] A group of workers in slate-dust cloaks stood near the names table. Chalk in hand. Slates under arm. They watched hands, not faces.\n[L3] A message runner arrived from the upper terrace with a sealed tube. The tube was passed to a clerk, then to the factor.\n[L4] The factor broke the seal. The paper was read. The paper was folded.\n[L5] On the platform, an administrator in a clean coat stepped to the speaking rail. A glass horn was adjusted to align with his mouth.\n[L6] At the names table, a clerk dipped a charcoal stick and wrote faster.\n[L7] A bell struck once. A pause.\n[L8] The stadium bowl quieted.\n[L9] Chapter 14 — Tiffani (The Briefing)\n[L10] The stadium was not built for this many bodies at once.\n[L11] It had been built for contests.\n[L12] Now the stone tiers held families and crates and brace timber. People sat with tools across their knees because putting tools down felt like letting go of the only reliable thing.\n[L13] Tiffani took a position where she could see the platform and one of the main aisle lines. She kept her slate on her lap. Closed. Not because she was not recording. Because she was choosing what would not be stolen.\n[L14] The horn-glass amplifiers had been assembled on the platform: three flared cones of smoked glass mounted in iron frames, their throats wrapped in cloth to reduce vibration. Copper wire bracing ran from the frames to anchors drilled into the stone.\n[L15] The wire shivered on the doubled hum.\n[L16] A bell cadence ran again from the nearest station tower, shorter this time, a confirmation sequence.\n[L17] Three short. One long. One short.\n[L18] Attention.\n[L19] A crown guard line tightened along the platform edge. Interns were mixed in with full guards. Bram was easy to identify by absence of stillness. He shifted, then corrected, then shifted again like he was trying to find a posture that did not look like a boy wearing someone else's authority.\n[L20] Jhace stood one tier down and two rows over, wedge bar tucked under his arm like a crutch he refused to admit he needed. Chloen was beside him, shoulders drawn in, eyes scanning for exits even though this place had too many.\n[L21] A man stepped onto the platform.\n[L22] Clean coat. Straight seams. An iron emblem pinned at the collar: a square frame with a single diagonal bar. Form.\n[L23] He did not wait for applause.\n[L24] He placed both hands on the podium rail and began.\n[L25] \"Notice from the Crown Council on Infrastructure and Public Safety,\" he said.\n[L26] The horn-glass carried his voice across stone. It arrived in fragments where the hum interfered and clean where the cloth wraps held.\n[L27] \"Chief Administrator Corlexi,\" he continued. \"Office of Infrastructure and Public Works. Forty-eight hour situation report. Verified items only.\"\n[L28] Tiffani opened her slate.\n[L29] She wrote the time interval in the corner and drew a line under it.\n[L30] Corlexi spoke in numbered sections.\n[L31] \"One: Communication status. The long-range grid is nonfunctional in most territories.\" He paused, allowing the crowd to hear the phrase most territories and supply its own meaning. \"Metal infrastructure is carrying the new harmonic. It is not usable for transmission. Attempts to force signal through have resulted in line fracture and fire.\"\n[L32] Tiffani wrote: GRID 98% FAIL (REPORT). CONDUCTOR BEHAVIOR.\n[L33] \"Local bell networks remain effective within city limits. Cadence corruption has been observed. If your station tower issues overlapping sequences, defer to the longest strike. The long strike indicates verified notice.\"\n[L34] A brace captain two tiers down nodded once, sharp, repeating the instruction to the men near him.\n# 【3†confluence_novella_revised.md†file_00000000a47071fdaf1ac40e7e480e92】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: confluence_novella_revised.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn16file3\nMclick Target: \"16:3\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] He did not understand what had happened. His eyes widened and then locked back into his assigned scan.\n[L2] A worker carrying a crate did not recover.\n[L3] His foot landed on a stone lip that had been stable one beat ago. The lip shifted under the stutter. The crate tilted. His weight followed.\n[L4] He went down.\n[L5] The crate broke.\n[L6] Glass shards and iron bolts scattered.\n[L7] The worker's forearm struck a jagged edge.\n[L8] Blood hit stone.\n[L9] Tiffani's pencil moved without permission.\n[L10] BLOOD. LOCATION: AISLE 3. CAUSE: PHASE NULL / LOAD SHIFT.\n[L11] She wrote around the red as if it were a stain on the page. Her lines tightened. Her letters narrowed.\n[L12] The hum returned.\n[L13] The slack phase arrived late.\n[L14] Air rushed back into lungs. The crowd noise surged, then splintered into shouts.\n[L15] Guards moved. Brace captains moved. Two medics pushed through with a cloth roll and a splint.\n[L16] Bram's face went pale for one heartbeat, then he stepped aside to make a lane. He did not joke. He did not freeze. He held the rope post steady while bodies passed.\n[L17] Jhace did not move toward the blood. He watched the brace timber and the shifting stone and the way the stutter had changed the crowd's balance. His hands flexed once, then clenched.\n[L18] Chloen stood with her arms tight around herself, eyes on the aisle line. Her mouth worked once as if the metallic taste was too loud.\n[L19] Corlexi remained on the platform.\n[L20] He did not return to the podium.\n[L21] He did not speak.\n[L22] His clean coat did not change.\n[L23] A registrar leaned in to say something to him. Corlexi nodded once, sharp, and pointed toward the side table.\n[L24] The ledger opened.\n[L25] Names began to be taken.\n[L26] Tiffani closed her slate.\n[L27] Not because the blood was not data.\n[L28] Because the act of writing had just become part of the event.\n[L29] She could feel the graphite under her fingers like a conductor.\n[L30] She stood and shifted one step to spare her knee.\n[L31] She did not look at Bram.\n[L32] She did not look at Jhace.\n[L33] She looked at the horn-glass amplifiers and the copper wire bracing and the way the wire shivered on the baseline as if it was listening.\n[L34] Then she exhaled on the slack phase and moved toward the aisle line where the blood had been, because measurement required proximity and proximity now carried cost.\n[L35] Chapter 15 – Tiffani (The Omission)\n[L36] The bells rang the Assembly cadence.\n[L37] Not alarm, evacuation, or mourning.\n[L38] Instruction.\n[L39] Three low. One high. Pause. Then the repeat—slightly off, like everything else now. The harmonic bled into the bronze, stretched the spacing between notes until the rhythm felt learned rather than remembered.\n[L40] Tiffani stood where she could see the whole of it without being seen herself.\n[L41] The crowd had been arranged for sound, not comfort. That was deliberate. Stone risers angled shallow toward the dais. Rope lines kept bodies from pressing too close to the glasswork that carried the amplification. Brace captains flanked the aisles, hands loose, eyes working.\n[L42] People had stopped talking before the bells finished.\n[L43] That mattered.\n[L44] They were waiting to be told what the Event was.\n[L45] Corlexi stood already at the center of the platform when the last echo died. No procession. No flourish. He wore Crown gray—clean lines, no ornament—and the way he held himself said he had practiced stillness until it read as calm instead of fear.\n[L46] Tiffani opened her slate.\n[L47] Not to write yet. To feel the grit along the edge lift and settle. Baseline check. It answered.\n[L48] Corlexi began.\n# 【4†The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt†file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn16file4\nMclick Target: \"16:4\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] Jhace wasn’t in the bowl anymore.\r\n[L2] She noticed that absence the way she noticed missing stairs—by almost stepping where something should have been.\r\n[L3] The emblems on coats and banners had finished settling. Square near the front. Loop nearer the lanes. Drop along the water tables. Fracture pushed outward, closer to the exits.\r\n[L4] That wasn’t how they’d been.\r\n[L5] Chloen didn’t know how she knew the difference. She just knew the shapes had moved the way animals moved when you weren’t watching them directly.\r\n[L6] A brace captain called for hands near the west rib. Chloen drifted with the crowd until she wasn’t near Tiffani anymore, then stopped.\r\n[L7] No one noticed.\r\n[L8] That was the trick.\r\n[L9] She stood where two rope lanes crossed and watched how people flowed around the knot. One lane moved faster. The other slowed and backed up. People grew impatient and leaned, and the lean pushed the timing off by half a beat.\r\n[L10] Half a beat mattered now.\r\n[L11] Chloen stepped sideways.\r\n[L12] Just one pace. Not dramatic. Just enough to stand where the knot wanted to form.\r\n[L13] The next pull came.\r\n[L14] People hesitated. The lane loosened. The knot didn’t catch.\r\n[L15] No one fell.\r\n[L16] No one noticed her.\r\n[L17] Chloen’s heart thudded too fast, then slowed when the slack phase came like it was supposed to.\r\n[L18] She hadn’t meant to do anything.\r\n[L19] She just didn’t like how the ropes were talking to the crowd.\r\n[L20] She drifted back toward the stone flats and found the low wall again. Sat. Hugged her elbows. Checked her mouth.\r\n[L21] Still no metal.\r\n[L22] That was worse.\r\n[L23] Tiffani passed close enough that Chloen could hear the pencil scratch. The graphite moved cleanly now, not skirting smears, not hesitating.\r\n[L24] Chloen wanted to say something. Anything.\r\n[L25] She didn’t.\r\n[L26] Across the stadium, Eries moved among the lanes without hurry. People made space for her without being told. She nodded. She listened. She touched shoulders lightly and left them straighter than before.\r\n[L27] She didn’t look at Chloen.\r\n[L28] Which meant she knew exactly where Chloen was.\r\n[L29] The bells rang notice cadence again—short, short, long—and this time Chloen breathed shallow before she remembered she was supposed to.\r\n[L30] Her body had learned faster than she had.\r\n[L31] She pressed her thumb into her palm until it hurt, just to make sure pain still worked the same way.\r\n[L32] It did.\r\n[L33] Good.\r\n[L34] A woman nearby whispered, “It’s better already,” and someone else answered, “Thank the Crown,” and a third said, “Thank her,” without needing to name who.\r\n[L35] Chloen watched the words move through the air like dust settling into cracks.\r\n[L36] She licked her teeth again.\r\n[L37] Nothing.\r\n[L38] She imagined the taste was still there, just buried deeper. Like grit ground into stone so fine you didn’t feel it until later.\r\n[L39] Chloen slid off the wall and followed the edge of the stadium where shadows cut the lines and the bells sounded farther away.\r\n[L40] She didn’t know what would happen next.\r\n[L41] She only knew this:\r\n[L42] If the world stopped hurting you right away, it didn’t mean it was healed.\r\n[L43] Sometimes it meant you’d stopped feeling the damage.\r\n[L44] Chloen kept walking, quiet and uncounted, while the city learned how to behave.\r\n[L45] Epilogue — Corlexi\r\n[L46] Corlexi did not return to the floor.\r\n[L47] He took the side stair instead—the one cut narrow for servants and clerks, stone worn smooth by centuries of people who were never meant to be seen leaving power. The bells were still ringing when he reached the landing, their cadence settling into something regular enough to pretend it had always been that way.\r\n[L48] He paused with one hand on the rail.\r\n[L49] Listened.\r\n[L50] The doubled hum had stabilized. Not vanished.\n# 【5†confluence_novella_revised.md†file_00000000a47071fdaf1ac40e7e480e92】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: confluence_novella_revised.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn16file5\nMclick Target: \"16:5\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] The guard captain wore a tab with the crown mark at the collar.\n[L2] A group of workers in slate-dust cloaks stood near the names table. Chalk in hand. Slates under arm. They watched hands, not faces.\n[L3] A message runner arrived from the upper terrace with a sealed tube. The tube was passed to a clerk, then to the factor.\n[L4] The factor broke the seal. The paper was read. The paper was folded.\n[L5] On the platform, an administrator in a clean coat stepped to the speaking rail. A glass horn was adjusted to align with his mouth.\n[L6] At the names table, a clerk dipped a charcoal stick and wrote faster.\n[L7] A bell struck once. A pause.\n[L8] The stadium bowl quieted.\n[L9] Chapter 14 — Tiffani (The Briefing)\n[L10] The stadium was not built for this many bodies at once.\n[L11] It had been built for contests.\n[L12] Now the stone tiers held families and crates and brace timber. People sat with tools across their knees because putting tools down felt like letting go of the only reliable thing.\n[L13] Tiffani took a position where she could see the platform and one of the main aisle lines. She kept her slate on her lap. Closed. Not because she was not recording. Because she was choosing what would not be stolen.\n[L14] The horn-glass amplifiers had been assembled on the platform: three flared cones of smoked glass mounted in iron frames, their throats wrapped in cloth to reduce vibration. Copper wire bracing ran from the frames to anchors drilled into the stone.\n[L15] The wire shivered on the doubled hum.\n[L16] A bell cadence ran again from the nearest station tower, shorter this time, a confirmation sequence.\n[L17] Three short. One long. One short.\n[L18] Attention.\n[L19] A crown guard line tightened along the platform edge. Interns were mixed in with full guards. Bram was easy to identify by absence of stillness. He shifted, then corrected, then shifted again like he was trying to find a posture that did not look like a boy wearing someone else's authority.\n[L20] Jhace stood one tier down and two rows over, wedge bar tucked under his arm like a crutch he refused to admit he needed. Chloen was beside him, shoulders drawn in, eyes scanning for exits even though this place had too many.\n[L21] A man stepped onto the platform.\n[L22] Clean coat. Straight seams. An iron emblem pinned at the collar: a square frame with a single diagonal bar. Form.\n[L23] He did not wait for applause.\n[L24] He placed both hands on the podium rail and began.\n[L25] \"Notice from the Crown Council on Infrastructure and Public Safety,\" he said.\n[L26] The horn-glass carried his voice across stone. It arrived in fragments where the hum interfered and clean where the cloth wraps held.\n[L27] \"Chief Administrator Corlexi,\" he continued. \"Office of Infrastructure and Public Works. Forty-eight hour situation report. Verified items only.\"\n[L28] Tiffani opened her slate.\n[L29] She wrote the time interval in the corner and drew a line under it.\n[L30] Corlexi spoke in numbered sections.\n[L31] \"One: Communication status. The long-range grid is nonfunctional in most territories.\" He paused, allowing the crowd to hear the phrase most territories and supply its own meaning. \"Metal infrastructure is carrying the new harmonic. It is not usable for transmission. Attempts to force signal through have resulted in line fracture and fire.\"\n[L32] Tiffani wrote: GRID 98% FAIL (REPORT). CONDUCTOR BEHAVIOR.\n[L33] \"Local bell networks remain effective within city limits. Cadence corruption has been observed. If your station tower issues overlapping sequences, defer to the longest strike. The long strike indicates verified notice.\"\n[L34] A brace captain two tiers down nodded once, sharp, repeating the instruction to the men near him.\n# 【6†confluence_novella_revised.md†file_00000000a47071fdaf1ac40e7e480e92】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: confluence_novella_revised.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn16file6\nMclick Target: \"16:6\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] A scaffold stood at the center platform: pipe frame, strap joints, wedge pins. Glass horns were mounted on crossbars, their mouths aimed at the stone tiers. A second set of horns faced outward to carry sound beyond the stadium.\n[L2] A table line formed behind the platform. Three tables. One for water cups. One for bandage cloth and salve tins. One for names.\n[L3] At the names table, clerks held ledgers and charcoal sticks. A runner stood beside them with a bag of wax tabs. A guard watched the table with a hand on a baton.\n[L4] Those who balked at giving names were redirected to the far stair under guard—no shouting, no debate, just a rope turned into a gate.\n[L5] Two brace captains walked the seating tiers with chalk, marking cracks and posting rope across unstable rows. A third brace captain stood near the center platform with a rolled map and a wedge bar.\n[L6] Pipe quarter engineers moved along the scaffold frame, tapping pipe with a rod, listening, then tightening strap joints. One engineer carried a small pressure gauge in a glass case. The needle jittered on the doubled hum.\n[L7] A forge-guild crew in soot-stained coats assembled a portable brazier near the center platform. They heated rivets. They drove them into a cracked crossbar. Steam hissed where water hit hot metal.\n[L8] Priests in four emblems stood at different edges of the platform.\n[L9] A square emblem on a clean cloth tab.\n[L10] A droplet emblem on a dark strip of leather.\n[L11] A loop emblem on braided cord.\n[L12] A fractured line emblem on a torn sash.\n[L13] They did not stand together.\n[L14] A factor with rings on both hands took a seat at the platform rail. Two assistants stood behind him with folded papers. A crown guard captain stood at his shoulder.\n[L15] A second guard captain walked the aisle lanes and pointed groups into sections. The guard captain wore a tab with the crown mark at the collar.\n[L16] A group of workers in slate-dust cloaks stood near the names table. Chalk in hand. Slates under arm. They watched hands, not faces.\n[L17] A message runner arrived from the upper terrace with a sealed tube. The tube was passed to a clerk, then to the factor.\n[L18] The factor broke the seal. The paper was read. The paper was folded.\n[L19] On the platform, an administrator in a clean coat stepped to the speaking rail. A glass horn was adjusted to align with his mouth.\n[L20] At the names table, a clerk dipped a charcoal stick and wrote faster.\n[L21] A bell struck once. A pause.\n[L22] The stadium bowl quieted.\n[L23] Chapter 14 — Tiffani (The Briefing)\n[L24] The stadium was not built for this many bodies at once.\n[L25] It had been built for contests.\n[L26] Now the stone tiers held families and crates and brace timber. People sat with tools across their knees because putting tools down felt like letting go of the only reliable thing.\n[L27] Tiffani took a position where she could see the platform and one of the main aisle lines. She kept her slate on her lap. Closed. Not because she was not recording. Because she was choosing what would not be stolen.\n[L28] The horn-glass amplifiers had been assembled on the platform: three flared cones of smoked glass mounted in iron frames, their throats wrapped in cloth to reduce vibration. Copper wire bracing ran from the frames to anchors drilled into the stone.\n[L29] The wire shivered on the doubled hum.\n[L30] A bell cadence ran again from the nearest station tower, shorter this time, a confirmation sequence.\n[L31] Three short. One long. One short.\n[L32] Attention.\n[L33] A crown guard line tightened along the platform edge. Interns were mixed in with full guards. Bram was easy to identify by absence of stillness.\n# 【7†The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt†file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn16file7\nMclick Target: \"16:7\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] Jhace did not move toward the blood. Her mouth worked once as if the metallic taste was too loud.\r\n[L3] Corlexi remained on the platform.\r\n[L4] He did not return to the podium.\r\n[L5] He did not speak.\r\n[L6] His clean coat did not change.\r\n[L7] A registrar leaned in to say something to him. Corlexi nodded once, sharp, and pointed toward the side table.\r\n[L8] The ledger opened.\r\n[L9] Names began to be taken.\r\n[L10] Tiffani closed her slate.\r\n[L11] Not because the blood was not data.\r\n[L12] Because the act of writing had just become part of the event.\r\n[L13] She could feel the graphite under her fingers like a conductor.\r\n[L14] She stood and shifted one step to spare her knee.\r\n[L15] She did not look at Bram.\r\n[L16] She did not look at Jhace.\r\n[L17] She looked at the horn-glass amplifiers and the copper wire bracing and the way the wire shivered on the baseline as if it was listening.\r\n[L18] Then she exhaled on the slack phase and moved toward the aisle line where the blood had been, because measurement required proximity and proximity now carried cost.\r\n[L19] Chapter 15 – Tiffani (The Omission)\r\n[L20] The bells rang the Assembly cadence.\r\n[L21] Not alarm. Brace captains flanked the aisles, hands loose, eyes working.\r\n[L26] People had stopped talking before the bells finished.\r\n[L27] That mattered.\r\n[L28] They were waiting to be told what the Event was.\r\n[L29] Corlexi stood already at the center of the platform when the last echo died. No procession. No flourish. He wore Crown gray—clean lines, no ornament—and the way he held himself said he had practiced stillness until it read as calm instead of fear.\r\n[L30] Tiffani opened her slate.\r\n[L31] Not to write yet. To feel the grit along the edge lift and settle. Baseline check. It answered.\r\n[L32] Corlexi began.\r\n[L33] “Citizens of Saint Merro and the upper terraces,” he said, voice carried clean through the glass horns. “We are convened under conditions without precedent.”\r\n[L34] That was true. Also useless.\r\n[L35] “We have experienced a planetary structural failure event,” he continued. “External in origin. Sudden. Unpreventable.”\r\n[L36] External. Tiffani underlined it in her mind and waited for the evidence that would follow.\r\n[L37] “It has compromised the grid,” Corlexi said. “Altered pressure dynamics. Introduced sustained harmonic instability.”\r\n[L38] He was careful with verbs. Passive constructions. No actor named.\r\n[L39] The crowd stayed quiet. Too quiet. People leaned forward a fraction with each pulse, then settled back on the slack. They had learned the rhythm faster than she’d expected.\r\n[L40] Corlexi spoke of logistics.\r\n[L41] Assembly points. Rationing. Repair priorities. The need for order while assessment continued. He named the glass-houses—plural—and acknowledged the Underface without promising aid he couldn’t deliver.\r\n[L42] All of it was competent.\r\n[L43] All of it was insufficient.\r\n[L44] Tiffani waited for him to say how big.\r\n[L45] She waited for where else.\n# 【8†The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt†file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn16file8\nMclick Target: \"16:8\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] He did not like sloppy.\r\n[L2] A guard captain in a crown tab passed and nodded at him once. \"Intern. Keep them moving.\"\r\n[L3] \"Yes, sir,\" Bram said.\r\n[L4] The captain moved on.\r\n[L5] Bram glanced back toward the section he'd placed them in.\r\n[L6] He found them by shape.\r\n[L7] Jhace: standing, wedge bar still in hand like a limb.\r\n[L8] Tiffani: sitting, slate closed on her knees, eyes forward.\r\n[L9] Chloen: small in the seat, shoulders tight, eyes too big for her face.\r\n[L10] Bram felt something in his chest that wasn't doubled hum and wasn't prayer. Just weight.\r\n[L11] He wanted to go back and say: sorry. He wanted to say: you don't have to answer me. He wanted to say: if you need water, I can get water.\r\n[L12] He didn't.\r\n[L13] Because guards didn't fix losses. Guards moved bodies.\r\n[L14] He kept walking the lanes.\r\n[L15] A bell struck the notice cadence again. Three short. Pause. One long. Two short.\r\n[L16] People quieted in waves.\r\n[L17] Bram stopped at the edge of a rope lane and watched the crowd settle into something like attention.\r\n[L18] He watched Chloen stand when the people around her stood, then sit when they sat, matching the tide of bodies with the same careful invisibility she'd carried all day.\r\n[L19] He watched her look once over her shoulder, not at him, just back at the path they'd walked as if checking whether the question had followed.\r\n[L20] He felt guilty anyway.\r\n[L21] He told himself he'd do better.\r\n[L22] He told himself he'd ask fewer questions.\r\n[L23] He told himself he'd keep his jokes aimed at the sky, not at kids.\r\n[L24] Then he saw the platform at the center of the stadium and the glass horns angled toward the stands, and he felt the larger truth settle in.\r\n[L25] Today was not going to stop handing him chances to fail.\r\n[L26] Chapter 13 - Camera (The Bowl)\r\n[L27] Bell stations at four corners of the stadium bowl rang the notice cadence in sequence.\r\n[L28] Guards raised ropes to open lanes. Guards lowered ropes to close lanes.\r\n[L29] Crowds entered by terrace ramps and split at chalk marks into sections. Cloth tabs tied to wrists matched section colors. Red, blue, grey, raw twine.\r\n[L30] A scaffold stood at the center platform: pipe frame, strap joints, wedge pins. Glass horns were mounted on crossbars, their mouths aimed at the stone tiers. A second set of horns faced outward to carry sound beyond the stadium.\r\n[L31] A table line formed behind the platform. Three tables. One for water cups. One for bandage cloth and salve tins. One for names.\r\n[L32] At the names table, clerks held ledgers and charcoal sticks. A runner stood beside them with a bag of wax tabs. A guard watched the table with a hand on a baton.\r\n[L33] Two brace captains walked the seating tiers with chalk, marking cracks and posting rope across unstable rows. A third brace captain stood near the center platform with a rolled map and a wedge bar.\r\n[L34] Pipe quarter engineers moved along the scaffold frame, tapping pipe with a rod, listening, then tightening strap joints. One engineer carried a small pressure gauge in a glass case. The needle jittered on the doubled hum.\r\n[L35] A forge-guild crew in soot-stained coats assembled a portable brazier near the center platform. They heated rivets. They drove them into a cracked crossbar. Steam hissed where water hit hot metal.\r\n[L36] Priests in four emblems stood at different edges of the platform.\r\n[L37] A square emblem on a clean cloth tab.\r\n[L38] A droplet emblem on a dark strip of leather.\r\n[L39] A loop emblem on braided cord.\r\n[L40] A fractured line emblem on a torn sash.\r\n[L41] They did not stand together.\r\n[L42] A factor with rings on both hands took a seat at the platform rail.\n# 【9†The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt†file_0000000023fc7230b413bd6905a776b9】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: The Confluence Chronicles (Chapters 1-18 & Epilogue).txt\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn16file9\nMclick Target: \"16:9\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] Bring injured if transportable. Bring names for allocation.\"\r\n[L2] A second runner repeated it down the street, voice already hoarse.\r\n[L3] Tiffani did not write it. She copied the timing in her head.\r\n[L4] Corlexi.\r\n[L5] She had heard the name once in the glass-house, spoken with irritation by an older observer who disliked administrators. She had never seen him.\r\n[L6] The crowd broke into motion in fragments.\r\n[L7] People argued. People complied. People dragged carts. People carried children. Rope lines formed across cracks as if the street itself had become a ledger.\r\n[L8] Tiffani stepped to the slack phase and walked.\r\n[L9] Jhace moved with her without asking.\r\n[L10] Chloen moved because the bodies around her moved.\r\n[L11] At the next station, the same cadence rang. The same sheet hung. The same order.\r\n[L12] The Crown could not speak across the grid anymore, but it could still ring metal.\r\n[L13] Tiffani tracked the failures that made that true.\r\n[L14] Long-range line: dead. The doubled harmonic rode the pipes and ate signal. Brass joints chattered under the pulse. Copper runs sang into noise.\r\n[L15] Local bell: usable. Barely.\r\n[L16] Messenger: human bandwidth.\r\n[L17] She kept those columns in her head the way she kept star positions.\r\n[L18] They reached a wider street where the terraces flattened and the stone was less fractured. A guard post had been set at the corner with a temporary marker: a pole, a hanging lantern, a scrap of white cloth tied in a knot.\r\n[L19] A young guard stood beside it. Crown colors on his sash, but the sash was too new, the stitching too bright. Helmet under one arm. Eyes tired anyway.\r\n[L20] He lifted a hand to stop the flow, then thought better of it and stepped aside to guide instead.\r\n[L21] \"Stadium route,\" he called. \"Keep to the marked flats. Avoid the red chalk. If you see rope, you do not step over it.\"\r\n[L22] His voice tried for authority and came out as practice.\r\n[L23] Tiffani watched his posture. Not trained yet. Assigned anyway.\r\n[L24] He spotted Jhace's wedge bar and grinned like he'd found something that made sense.\r\n[L25] \"Good,\" the guard said. \"Tools. Love a man who brings his own teeth to the problem.\"\r\n[L26] Jhace's mouth tightened. Not a smile. Not not.\r\n[L27] The guard's grin widened as if that was permission.\r\n[L28] \"Name's Bram. Interning with the Crown. Which is a polite way of saying they put me where the stone falls and hope I learn fast.\"\r\n[L29] He paused, then added, \"I did not choose the phrase 'interning with the Crown.' The phrase chose me.\"\r\n[L30] Tiffani felt her knee register load and adjusted her stride. Bram's voice was noise in the measurement.\r\n[L31] Jhace looked at Bram's sash. \"Intern,\" he repeated.\r\n[L32] \"Yes.\" Bram nodded too hard. \"Like a nail that isn't sure if it's headed for wood or thumb.\"\r\n[L33] He started to wave them forward, then stopped mid-gesture like he could hear his own metaphor.\r\n[L34] \"That one was bad,\" Bram said. \"Forget I said thumb. The Crown would prefer no thumbs were mentioned.\"\r\n[L35] Jhace's eyes narrowed.\r\n[L36] \"Finish it,\" he said.\r\n[L37] Bram blinked. \"What?\"\r\n[L38] \"The joke,\" Jhace said. \"You started it. You finish it.\"\r\n[L39] Bram's mouth opened, closed, opened again. \"It's not a joke. It's a warning. A poorly hammered warning.\"\r\n[L40] \"Still,\" Jhace said.\r\n[L41] Tiffani looked away from both of them. The doubled hum under the street held steady. The air pulsed thin-thick. This mattered more than whatever Bram was doing with words.\nVisible: 0% - 100%",
